violations.text: The grout in the warewashing area is in poor repair. The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 04/28/2026

Establishment did not have an employee present at the time of inspection that has taken an approved food safety managers course. Establishment must have an employee certified in food safety at all times when time temperature control for safety (TCS) food is being prepared, handled, and/or served. Per owner, establishment must enroll additional employees in a food safety manager course. Please email inspector proof of enrollment. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 10/29/2025
A dish rag was observed in the hand-washing sink. A hand-washing sink must not be used for purposes other than hand washing. Corrected On-Site. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 10/15/2025

Quipes and chulitos were observed held at 73-88 degrees Fahrenheit in the hot hold unit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be maintained at 135 degrees Fahrenheit or above except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Per person in charge, food had been placed in the hot hold unit about 1.5 hours after being cooked. Food was reheated to 176 and 171 degrees Fahrenheit and placed back in the hot hold unit during time of inspection. The ambient air temperature was observed at 155 degrees Fahrenheit. Per person in charge, food is placed in the hot hold unit before it is heated up. Establishment must preheat up the hot hold unit to 135 degrees Fahrenheit or above before placing food in it. Establishment must record food temperatures and send them to inspector daily. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 10/15/2025

The screen door to the outside, located in the kitchen, is not tight-fitting. Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 10/22/2025

The presence of cockroaches is not controlled as evidenced by cockroaches in the warewashing area. The presence of insects, rodents, and other pests must be controlled by routinely inspecting the premises for evidence of pests, using acceptable methods to control pests, eliminating harborage conditions, and routinely inspecting incoming shipments of food and supplies. Pest control records were reviewed during time of inspection. Establishment must schedule pest control service and come up with a pest control plan. Please email inspector pest control reports. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 10/31/2025

The condensate from the air conditioning unit in the kitchen and back room are draining into a container. Condensate drainage and other nonsewage liquids must drain into a drain. Per person in charge, repair has been scheduled. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 10/29/2025
The FPR wall in the warewashing area is in poor repair (not tight fitting). The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 11/11/2025
The coving in the warewashing area is in poor repair (not tight fitting). The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 11/11/2025
The grout in the warewashing area is in poor repair. The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 04/28/2026

Establishment did not have an employee present at the time of inspection that has taken an approved food safety managers course. Establishment must have an employee certified in food safety at all times when time temperature control for safety (TCS) food is being prepared, handled, and/or served. Establishment must enroll additional employees in a food safety manager course. Please email inspector proof of enrollment. New Violation. Correct By: 10/29/2025
A cup was observed in the hand-washing sink. A hand-washing sink must not be used for purposes other than hand washing. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 10/15/2025

Empanadas and chulitos were observed held at 92-129 degrees Fahrenheit in the hot hold unit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be maintained at 135 degrees Fahrenheit or above except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Per person in charge, food had been placed in the hot hold unit about 1.5 hours after being cooked. Food was reheated to above 165 degrees Fahrenheit and placed back in the hot hold unit during time of inspection. The initial ambient air temperature was observed at 113 degrees Fahrenheit. Hot hold unit temperature was adjusted and observed at 218 degrees Fahrenheit after adjustment. Per person in charge, food is placed in the hot hold unit before it is heated up. Establishment must preheat up the hot hold unit to 135 degrees Fahrenheit or above before placing food in it. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 10/15/2025

Baked goods packaged in the food establishment are lacking labels. Food packaged in a food establishment must be labeled with the common name of the food, a list of ingredients, the quantity of the contents, and the business name/address. Baked goods were removed from the shelf and placed behind the counter at time of inspection. Establishment must not make baked good available for self-serve until labels are made. Please email inspector labels. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 10/22/2025

The door to the outside, located in the the kitchen , is not tight-fitting. Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. New Violation. Correct By: 10/22/2025
The condensate from the air conditioning unit is draining into a handwashing sink. Condensate drainage and other non-sewage liquids must drain into a drain. New Violation. Correct By: 10/29/2025

Raw animal foods (whole shell eggs) were observed stored above ready-to-eat foods (fresh fruit, bottled beverages) in the beverage refrigerator. Raw animal foods must be separated from raw ready-to-eat food such as vegetables during storage. New Violation. Correct By: 01/08/2024
Several cans of insecticide (Raid roach spray) were observed in the establishment. PIC informed to cease use. New Violation. Correct By: 01/08/2024
Cleaned and sanitized equipment were not allowed to air dry. Sheet pans were observed stacked together without drying. New Violation. Correct By: 01/08/2024

The non-food contact surfaces of the fan guards on the exhaust fan, fan in dish room, and walk-in have an accumulation of dust, dirt, food residue, and other debris. New Violation. Correct By: 01/08/2024
The non-food contact surfaces of the shelves in the dry storage room have an accumulation of food residue, and other debris. New Violation. Correct By: 01/08/2024
The condensate from the air conditioning unit is draining into a handwashing sink. Condensate drainage and other non-sewage liquids must drain into a drain. New Violation. Correct By: 01/22/2024
